---
name: Feature Request
about: Suggest a new feature or enhancement for JCOSEK
title: '[FEATURE] '
labels: enhancement
assignees: ''
---

## Feature Description

<!-- A clear and concise description of the feature you would like to be added. -->

## Use Case / Problem Statement

<!-- Explain your use case. What specific problem does this feature solve? Who will benefit from it? -->

## Current Workaround / Alternatives

<!-- Describe any workarounds, alternative solutions, or temporary fixes you are currently using. -->

## Proposed Solution

<!-- Describe the architecture, API design, or implementation strategy you have in mind. -->

## Pseudocode or Code Examples

<!-- If applicable, provide code snippets or pseudocode showing how the proposed API or feature should be used. -->

```c
// Example usage of the proposed feature
void example_usage(void) {
    // Show how this new capability would blend into application code
}
```

## Performance & Resource Considerations

<!-- How will this feature affect performance? -->
<!-- Please specifically consider embedded resource constraints (Flash, RAM, CPU cycles, power consumption). -->

## Implementation Complexity

<!-- How complex do you anticipate this feature to be? -->
- [ ] Low (Simple typo fixes, peripheral configuration extensions, minor documentation additions)
- [ ] Medium (New driver module, localized architectural refactoring)
- [ ] High (Core OS kernel changes, scheduling algorithm overhauls, breaking API modifications)

## Related Issues

<!-- Link to any existing or related issues here if applicable. -->

## Additional Context

<!-- Add any other background information, link references, block diagrams, or screenshots here. -->

---

<!-- Pre-submission Checklist:
- [ ] I have searched existing Issues to ensure this is a new, unique feature request.
- [ ] I have provided a clear use case explaining why this feature is valuable.
- [ ] I have considered the performance and hardware resource impact of this feature.
- [ ] My description is detailed and easy for maintainers to understand.
-->
